Original Light Contract Demand.

pages.raw_text:

cred.


Why" he said, I'VE are ashamed
to tell other light men the low
price we get. 1t is the lowest in
the country and don't see any use
of all these suggestions. You do
not deny the lowness of price. Ma
terial now costs rs per cent more
than 5 years ago, when the contract
was first made. Copper wire has
advanced 100 per cent in price. In
fact, every item of expenditure is
higher.


Looking at it from cold
blooded standpoint, why should we
take a contract that would be prac-
tically profitless? We want to
know If our business is definite on
account of financial conditions.
I'VE have no desire to take the
city by the threat and will go on
lighting for awhile, at least"
Commissioner O'Brien then in
terrupted by suggesting that the
company might make shorter con-
tract If the city would agree to in
stall additional lights. Huntington
replied that it would greatly in
crease the cost to the city and
therefore would be a penny-wise-
pound-fOolish thing to do.
AS the matter now stands, the
Washington Water Power Co.
wants contract that each light
shall run years at S4S, no matter
when installed 1t will also sub
nit year proposition for lights
now installed at 81 per light per
month.


The city is also asked to state
just how many additional lights it
will need for the coming year, so
that the company may purchase
material and be ready to install
them as soon as possible after they
are ordered


AS the council committee has no
power to act there is nothing more
to the conference until report is
made to the city council.
